          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12822
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;tool-use data-tool-type=\"read\" data-tool-name=\"read_file_v2\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 12823
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Tool use: **read_file_v2** â˘ Read file: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/xtask_todo/crates/todo/Cargo.toml&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 12824
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12825
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 12826
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/tool-use&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 12827
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12828
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 12829
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12830
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 12831
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12832
          },
          {
            "text": "Now I have a clearer picture. The binary has:",
            "lineNumber": 12833
          },
          {
            "text": "- main.rs - CLI entry, load VFS, run REPL",
            "lineNumber": 12834
          },
          {
            "text": "- parser.rs - tokenize, parse Pipeline/SimpleCommand (pure logic, easy to unit test if we can run it)",
            "lineNumber": 12835
          },
          {
            "text": "- serialization.rs - load/save .bin format (pure logic + I/O)",
            "lineNumber": 12836
          },
          {
            "text": "- vfs.rs - virtual filesystem (Node, Vfs)",
            "lineNumber": 12837
          },
          {
            "text": "- command.rs - run_builtin_*, ExecContext (needs VFS, streams)",
            "lineNumber": 12838
          },
          {
            "text": "- repl.rs - read loop, dispatch to command",
            "lineNumber": 12839
          },
          {
            "text": "- completion.rs - rustyline completion",
            "lineNumber": 12840
          },
          {
            "text": "- todo_io.rs - load/save todos in devshell context",
            "lineNumber": 12841
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12842
          },
          {
            "text": "The key insight: **cargo test -p xtask-todo-lib** only builds and runs the **lib** and **integration tests**. The integration tests (in crates/todo/tests/) are separate binaries that link against the **lib**. So the binary (cargo_devshell) code is never linked into any test binary - it's only linked when you build the cargo-devshell binary. So when tarpaulin runs tests, the binary's code is never executed. That's why coverage is 21.77% (only the lib is exercised).",
            "lineNumber": 12843
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12844
          },
          {
            "text": "To get the binary code executed during &#96;cargo test&#96;, we have two approaches:",
            "lineNumber": 12845
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.15182049572467804
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./.specstory/history/2026-03-11_01-00Z-clippy.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1213
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1275
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1215
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1216
          },
          {
            "text": "_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_",
            "lineNumber": 1217
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1218
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1219
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1220
          },
          {
            "text": "The user wants to add a new cargo xtask command: &#96;cargo xtask git add&#96; that runs the equivalent of &#96;git add .github .specstory xtask docs&#96;. So I need to:",
            "lineNumber": 1221
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1222
          },
          {
            "text": "1. Add a new subcommand - but \"git add\" has a space. In argh, subcommands are typically single words. So we could do:",
            "lineNumber": 1223
          },
          {
            "text": "   - &#96;xtask git-add&#96; (one subcommand)",
            "lineNumber": 1224
          },
          {
            "text": "   - or &#96;xtask git&#96; with a nested &#96;add&#96; subcommand",
            "lineNumber": 1225
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1226
          },
          {
            "text": "   The user said \"cargo xtask git add\" - so they want the invocation to be &#96;cargo xtask git add&#96;. That means we need a \"git\" top-level subcommand with an \"add\" subcommand under it. Let me check - in argh we can have nested subcommands. So:",
            "lineNumber": 1227
          },
          {
            "text": "   - XtaskSub::Git(GitArgs) ",
            "lineNumber": 1228
          },
          {
            "text": "   - GitArgs has sub: GitSub",
            "lineNumber": 1229
          },
          {
            "text": "   - GitSub::Add(AddArgs) ",
            "lineNumber": 1230
          },
          {
            "text": "   Then cmd would be &#96;cargo xtask git add&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 1231
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1232
          },
          {
            "text": "   Or we could have a single subcommand \"git-add\" and the user runs &#96;cargo xtask git-add&#96;. The user specifically said \"cargo xtask git add\" so I'll implement nested: &#96;git&#96; with subcommand &#96;add&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 1233
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1234
          },
          {
            "text": "2. The \"add\" subcommand would run &#96;git add .github .specstory xtask docs&#96; - so we need to execute that as a process. We need to run from the workspace root. We can use Command::new(\"git\").args([\"add\", \".github\", \".specstory\", \"xtask\", \"docs\"]).status()",
            "lineNumber": 1235
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1236
          },
          {
            "text": "Let me read the current main.rs to see the exact structure and add the git add command.",
            "lineNumber": 1237
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1238
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1239
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1240
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1241
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1242
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1243
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1244
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1245
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1246
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1247
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;tool-use data-tool-type=\"read\" data-tool-name=\"read_file_v2\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1248
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Tool use: **read_file_v2** • Read file: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/xtask_todo/xtask/src/main.rs&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1249
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1250
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1251
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/tool-use&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1252
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1253
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1254
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1255
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1256
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1257
          },
          {
            "text": "I need to add:",
            "lineNumber": 1258
          },
          {
            "text": "1. XtaskSub::Git(GitArgs)",
            "lineNumber": 1259
          },
          {
            "text": "2. GitArgs with a subcommand GitSub",
            "lineNumber": 1260
          },
          {
            "text": "3. GitSub::Add(GitAddArgs) - can be empty struct",
            "lineNumber": 1261
          },
          {
            "text": "4. cmd_git that matches on GitSub::Add and runs git add .github .specstory xtask docs",
            "lineNumber": 1262
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1263
          },
          {
            "text": "Let me add the enum variant, the Git args struct, and the handler.",
            "lineNumber": 1264
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 1265
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1266

          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 3403
          },
          {
            "text": "The user wants me to split crates/todo/src/lib.rs because it has 688 lines (max 500). I need to read the file, understand its structure, and split it into smaller modules.",

          {

            "lineNumber": 3414

          {

          },
          {

          {

          {
            "text": "---",

          {
            "lineNumber": 3420
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 3421
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3422
          },
          {
            "text": "The file has 688 lines. I need to split it into modules so each file is under 500 lines. Logical split:",
            "lineNumber": 3423
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "text": "1. **id.rs** - TodoId (lines ~17-38) - small",
            "lineNumber": 3425
          },
          {
            "text": "2. **priority.rs** - Priority (lines ~40-80)",
            "lineNumber": 3426
          },
          {
            "text": "3. **repeat.rs** - RepeatRule (lines ~82-164)",
            "lineNumber": 3427
          },
          {
            "text": "4. **todo.rs** or **item.rs** - Todo, TodoPatch, ListFilter, ListSort, ListOptions (lines ~166-227)",
            "lineNumber": 3428
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 3429
          },

            "lineNumber": 3430
          },
          {
            "text": "7. **lib.rs** - mod declarations, re-exports, and maybe keep TodoList if it's the facade",
            "lineNumber": 3431
          },
          {
            "text": "8. **tests** - move to tests.rs or tests/mod.rs to keep lib under 500",
            "lineNumber": 3432
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3433
          },
          {
            "text": "Actually looking at the structure:",

4. cmd_gh runs `gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId'` then `gh run view <id> --log`

The user said the command is equivalent to:
gh run view $(gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId') --log

So we need to:
- Get the run ID: gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId'
- Then: gh run view <id> --log

If there are no runs, the first command might return empty or fail. We should handle that.

Let me complete the exploration and then ask one clarifying question. The scope is clear: one new subcommand `gh log`. Possible questions:
1. Should we support repo other than current (e.g. --repo owner/repo)?
2. If there are no runs, should we exit with a clear message and non-zero, or print nothing?
3. Should `gh` be a top-level subcommand with only `log` for now (extensible for future gh list, gh view, etc.)?

I'll ask one question at a time per the skill. First, the design is fairly straightforward - we're wrapping two gh calls. The main clarification could be: when there are no runs, what should happen? And should we depend on `gh` being installed (like git subcommand depends on git)?

I'll propose approaches and present the design. The skill says: propose 2-3 approaches, then present design in sections. So I don't have to ask many questions if the requirement is already clear. The user gave the exact equivalent command. So:
- Approach A: shell out to gh exactly as specified (two commands: get id, then gh run view <id> --log). Simple, requires gh in PATH.
- Approach B: same but in one shot with a shell (sh -c 'gh run view $(gh run list ...) --log'). Slightly less portable (subshell).
- Approach C: use GitHub API via HTTP (reqwest) to fetch run list and log. No gh dependency but more code and need to handle auth.

I'll go with exploring context done, then one short clarifying question: "当仓库没有 workflow run 时（例如新仓库或 gh 未登录），希望行为是：A 静默退出码 1 + 简短 stderr 说明；B 仅退出码 1 不输出；C 其他？"

当**没有可用的 workflow run** 时（例如新仓库、或 `gh` 未登录/未找到 run），你希望：

- **A**：退出码 1，并在 stderr 输出简短说明（例如 "no runs found" 或 "gh: not found"）  
- **B**：仅退出码 1，不向 stderr 输出  
- **C**：其他（请说明）

---


已记录：无 run 或 `gh` 不可用时，退出码 1 并在 stderr 输出简短说明。

下面给出 **2～3 种实现方式** 和推荐，然后分节写设计供你逐节确认。

---

## 方案概览

| 方案 | 做法 | 优点 | 缺点 |
|------|------|------|------|
| **A. 两次子进程调用** | 先 `gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId'` 取 run id，再 `gh run view <id> --log`；用 `Command` 输出捕获/转发，失败时 stderr 输出说明并返回 1。 | 与现有 `git` 子命令风格一致、逻辑清晰、易测。 | 需在 PATH 中找到 `gh`；两次进程。 |
| **B. 单次 shell** | 执行 `sh -c 'gh run view $(gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId -q ".[0].databaseId") --log'`，stdout/stderr 透传。 | 实现极简。 | 依赖 shell、引号/转义需小心、错误信息不如 A 好控制。 |
| **C. GitHub API** | 用 reqwest 调 GitHub API 取 run 列表与 log，不依赖 `gh`。 | 不要求安装 `gh`。 | 需处理 token、依赖与错误映射，实现量明显更大。 |

**推荐 A**：与 xtask 里 `git` 的用法一致，错误时便于在 stderr 写「no runs found」或「gh not found」等，且易做单元/集成测试。

---

## 设计草案（分节确认）

请按节回复「通过」或要改的点。

---

### 第 1 节：范围与依赖

- **命令**：`cargo xtask gh log`（顶层子命令 `gh`，子命令 `log`；暂不实现其他 `gh *`）。
- **依赖**：仅标准库 + 现有 `std::process::Command`；不新增 crate。前提：本机已安装 [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) 且已在 PATH 中（与「需要安装 git」类似）。
- **行为等价**：与  
  `gh run view $(gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId') --log`  
  一致：输出最近一次 run 的 job log 到 stdout；无 run 或 `gh` 失败时退出码 1 并在 stderr 输出简短说明（你选的 **A**）。

若同意，我按此写进设计说明（含「依赖与前提」小节）。

---

### 第 2 节：实现要点与错误处理

- **实现**：新增模块 `xtask/src/gh.rs`（及可选 `xtask/src/gh/log.rs`）；在 `lib.rs` 中注册 `GhArgs` 与 `XtaskSub::Gh`，分发到 `gh::cmd_gh`。`cmd_gh` 仅处理 `gh log`：先执行 `gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId'`，若 stdout 为空或非数字则视为「无 run」，stderr 写 "no runs found"（或类似）、返回 1；若有 id，再执行 `gh run view <id> --log`，stdout/stderr 透传到当前进程，若 `gh` 退出非零则 stderr 写错误信息并返回 1。
- **错误信息**：`gh` 未找到（`Command::new("gh")` 或 spawn 失败）→ stderr "gh: command not found"（或系统错误）；list 无结果或解析失败 → "no runs found"；view 失败 → 将 `gh run view` 的 stderr 转发或简短归纳。所有错误路径均为退出码 1 + stderr 说明（符合 **A**）。

若同意，我将在设计说明中固定上述实现要点与错误处理规则。

---

### 第 3 节：文档与测试

- **文档**：在 README 或 xtask 相关文档中增加一句：`cargo xtask gh log` 显示最近一次 GitHub Actions run 的 log，等价于上述 `gh run view ...` 命令；前提是已安装并配置 `gh`。
- **测试**：可选——在 CI 中不强制安装 `gh`，单元测试可 mock `Command` 或跳过「无 gh 环境」；若有集成测试，则仅在 `gh` 可用时运行并断言「有 run 时能拿到 log / 无 run 时退出 1 且 stderr 含预期说明」。

若同意，我按此写设计说明并注明「文档与测试」边界。

---

请按节回复：  
- 第 1 节（范围与依赖）：通过 / 或要改的点  
- 第 2 节（实现要点与错误处理）：通过 / 或要改的点  
- 第 3 节（文档与测试）：通过 / 或要改的点  

三节都通过后，我会把完整设计写入 `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-gh-log-design.md`，并进入 spec review / writing-plans（不直接写实现代码，遵守头脑风暴 HARD-GATE）。

---

1. Run `gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId'` and capture stdout
2. If failure, empty, or not a valid id -> stderr message, return Err
3. Run `gh run view <id> --log` with stdout/stderr inherited (not captured) so the user sees the log; if status != 0, return Err with stderr content or message

For (3), we want to "pass through" stdout/stderr to the current process. So we use .status() with stdio inherited (default), not .output(). So:
  let status = Command::new("gh").args(["run", "view", id_str, "--log"]).status()?;
  if !status.success() { ... }

For (1) we need to capture stdout:
  let out = Command::new("gh").args(["run", "list", "--limit", "1", "--json", "databaseId", "-q", ".[0].databaseId"]).output()?;
  if !out.status.success() { eprintln!("..."); return Err(...) }
  let id = String::from_utf8(out.stdout).trim().to_string();
  if id.is_empty() { eprintln!("no runs found"); return Err(...) }
  // id might be a number, we pass it to gh run view

Let me write gh.rs. We return Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> so that to_run_failure can be used in lib.
</details></think>

---





---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/xtask_todo/xtask/src/gh.rs</summary>

</details>

</tool-use>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

When we run `gh run view <id> --log`, we want the log to go to stdout of the current process. Right now we're using .status() which by default inherits stdin/stdout/stderr. So the log will already be printed to the user's terminal. Good.

One issue: when list fails we're printing to stderr but the design said we should also return Err so the process exits with 1. We do return Err. Good.

Another: the "no runs found" case - gh run list might return success but with an empty array, so the query .[0].databaseId could be empty or the output could be empty. We handle empty id. But what if the repo has no runs and gh returns something like "null"? Let me check - for an empty array, .[0].databaseId would be undefined in jq, so the output might be "null\n" or empty. So trim() and is_empty() covers that. We're good.

Fix the eprintln for list failure: we're using then_some(...).unwrap_or(...) - if msg.trim().is_empty() we print "gh run list failed", else we print msg.trim(). But we're passing the result to eprintln! so we need a single string. So: if msg.trim().is_empty() { eprintln!("gh run list failed") } else { eprintln!("{}", msg.trim()) }. Let me simplify.
</details></think>

---


修正 list 失败时的 stderr 输出逻辑。

---

**实现计划**  
已写入：`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-gh-log-impl-plan.md`

**代码与文档**

1. **`xtask/src/gh.rs`**（新文件）  
   - `GhArgs`（argh 子命令 `gh`）、`GhSub::Log(GhLogArgs)`、`cmd_gh`。  
   - `cmd_gh_log`：先执行 `gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId'` 取 run id；失败或 stdout 为空则 stderr 输出 "gh: command not found" / "gh run list failed" / "no runs found" 并返回 `Err`；有 id 则执行 `gh run view <id> --log`，stdout/stderr 透传；view 非零退出时 stderr 报错并返回 `Err`。

2. **`xtask/src/lib.rs`**  
   - 增加 `mod gh`、`use gh::GhArgs`、`XtaskSub::Gh(GhArgs)` 及 `run_with` 分支 `gh::cmd_gh(&args).map_err(to_run_failure)`。

3. **`README.md`**  
   - 在「Other xtask commands」表中增加一行：`cargo xtask gh log` 的说明与前提（需安装 GitHub CLI）。

**验证**  
- `cargo build -p xtask` 通过。  
- `cargo xtask gh --help` 显示 `log` 子命令。  
- 在本地有 run 时执行 `cargo xtask gh log` 会输出最近一次 Actions run 的 log。

可直接使用：`cargo xtask gh log`（需已安装并配置 `gh`）。

            "text": "//! TTY: rustyline Editor with path completion; non-TTY: &#96;read_line&#96; loop.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "//! On exit (exit/quit or EOF), VFS is auto-saved to &#96;bin_path&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "//! Shared loop body is in &#96;process_line&#96; so it can be unit-tested.",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::cell::RefCell;",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::io::{BufRead, Read, Write};",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::path::Path;",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::rc::Rc;",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "use rustyline::Editor;",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "use super::command::{execute_pipeline, ExecContext, RunResult};",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "use super::completion::DevShellHelper;",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "use super::parser;",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "use super::serialization;",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "use super::vfs::Vfs;",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Result of processing one REPL line: continue the loop or exit.",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "pub enum StepResult {",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    Continue,",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "    Exit,",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Process one input line: parse, optionally run pipeline, return whether to exit.",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Used by both TTY and non-TTY loops; exposed for tests.",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "pub fn process_line&lt;R, W1, W2&gt;(",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "    vfs: &amp;Rc&lt;RefCell&lt;Vfs&gt;&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "    line: &amp;str,",
            "lineNumber": 31
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.49793049693107605
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/command.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 50,
            "column": 32
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "//! Command execution: &#96;ExecContext&#96;, redirects, and builtin dispatch (pwd, cd, ls, mkdir, cat, touch, echo, export-readonly, save, todo, exit/quit).",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::io::Cursor;",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::io::{Read, Write};",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::path::Path;",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::path::PathBuf;",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "use super::parser::{Pipeline, SimpleCommand};",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "use super::serialization;",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "use super::todo_io::{list_from_todos, load_todos, save_todos};",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "use super::vfs::Vfs;",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "use crate::{InMemoryStore, TodoId, TodoList};",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Result of running a pipeline: continue the REPL loop or exit.",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "pub enum RunResult {",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "    Continue,",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "    Exit,",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Execution context: VFS and standard streams for one command.",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "pub struct ExecContext&lt;'a&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    pub vfs: &amp;'a mut Vfs,",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "    pub stdin: &amp;'a mut dyn Read,",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    pub stdout: &amp;'a mut dyn Write,",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "    pub stderr: &amp;'a mut dyn Write,",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Error from builtin execution (redirect or VFS failure, unknown command).",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "#[derive(Debug)]",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "pub enum BuiltinError {",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    UnknownCommand(String),",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "    RedirectRead,",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "    RedirectWrite,",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "    CdFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "    MkdirFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "    CatFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "    TouchFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "    LsFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "    ExportFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "    SaveFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    TodoLoadFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "    TodoSaveFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    TodoArgError,",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "    TodoDataError,",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Run a single command with given streams. Redirects override the provided stdin/stdout/stderr.",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "fn run_builtin_with_streams(",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "    vfs: &amp;mut Vfs,",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "    default_stdin: &amp;mut dyn Read",
            "lineNumber": 51
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.4954510033130646
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/command.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 129
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 178,
            "column": 16
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "pub fn execute_pipeline(",
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "text": "    ctx: &amp;mut ExecContext&lt;'_&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "text": "    pipeline: &amp;Pipeline,",
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; Result&lt;RunResult, BuiltinError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 133
          },
          {
            "text": "    let commands = &amp;pipeline.commands;",
            "lineNumber": 134
          },
          {
            "text": "    if commands.is_empty() {",
            "lineNumber": 135
          },
          {
            "text": "        return Ok(RunResult::Continue);",
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138
          },
          {
            "text": "    let first_argv0 = commands",
            "lineNumber": 139
          },
          {
            "text": "        .first()",
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "text": "        .and_then(|c| c.argv.first())",
            "lineNumber": 141
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map(String::as_str);",
            "lineNumber": 142
          },
          {
            "text": "    if first_argv0 == Some(\"exit\") || first_argv0 == Some(\"quit\") {",
            "lineNumber": 143
          },
          {
            "text": "        return Ok(RunResult::Exit);",
            "lineNumber": 144
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 145
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut prev_output: Option&lt;Vec&lt;u8&gt;&gt; = None;",
            "lineNumber": 147
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148
          },
          {
            "text": "    for (i, cmd) in commands.iter().enumerate() {",
            "lineNumber": 149
          },
          {
            "text": "        let is_first = i == 0;",
            "lineNumber": 150
          },
          {
            "text": "        let is_last = i == commands.len() - 1;",
            "lineNumber": 151
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152
          },
          {
            "text": "        let mut pipe_stdin: Option&lt;Cursor&lt;Vec&lt;u8&gt;&gt;&gt; = prev_output.take().map(Cursor::new);",
            "lineNumber": 153
          },
          {
            "text": "        let mut next_buffer = Vec::new();",
            "lineNumber": 154
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155
          },
          {
            "text": "        let stdin: &amp;mut dyn Read = if is_first {",
            "lineNumber": 156
          },
          {
            "text": "            ctx.stdin",
            "lineNumber": 157
          },
          {
            "text": "        } else {",
            "lineNumber": 158
          },
          {
            "text": "            pipe_stdin",
            "lineNumber": 159
          },
          {
            "text": "                .as_mut()",
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "text": "                .expect(\"non-first pipeline stage has pipe input\") as &amp;mut dyn Read",
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "        };",
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "text": "        let stdout: &amp;mut dyn Write = if is_last {",
            "lineNumber": 163
          },
          {
            "text": "            ctx.stdout",
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "text": "        } else {",
            "lineNumber": 165
          },
          {
            "text": "            &amp;mut next_buffer",
            "lineNumber": 166
          },
          {
            "text": "        };",
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168
          },
          {
            "text": "        run_builtin_with_streams(ctx.vfs, stdin, stdout, ctx.stderr, cmd)?;",
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "text": "        if !is_last {",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "            prev_output = Some(next_buffer);",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "text": "    Ok(RunResult::Continue)",
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "text": "fn run_todo_list",
            "lineNumber": 179
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.47653159499168396
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/command.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 403,
            "column": 27
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 461,
            "column": 92
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "    path: &amp;str,",
            "lineNumber": 405
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; Result&lt;(), BuiltinError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 406
          },
          {
            "text": "    let temp_base = std::env::temp_dir();",
            "lineNumber": 407
          },
          {
            "text": "    let subdir_name = format!(",
            "lineNumber": 408
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"dev_shell_export_{}_{}\",",
            "lineNumber": 409
          },
          {
            "text": "        std::process::id(),",
            "lineNumber": 410
          },
          {
            "text": "        std::time::SystemTime::now()",
            "lineNumber": 411
          },
          {
            "text": "            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)",
            "lineNumber": 412
          },
          {
            "text": "            .unwrap()",
            "lineNumber": 413
          },
          {
            "text": "            .as_millis()",
            "lineNumber": 414
          },
          {
            "text": "    );",
            "lineNumber": 415
          },
          {
            "text": "    let temp_dir = temp_base.join(&amp;subdir_name);",
            "lineNumber": 416
          },
          {
            "text": "    std::fs::create_dir_all(&amp;temp_dir).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::ExportFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 417
          },
          {
            "text": "    vfs.copy_tree_to_host(path, &amp;temp_dir)",
            "lineNumber": 418
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::ExportFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 419
          },
          {
            "text": "    let abs_path: PathBuf =",
            "lineNumber": 420
          },
          {
            "text": "        std::fs::canonicalize(&amp;temp_dir).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::ExportFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 421
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(stdout, \"{}\", abs_path.display()).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 422
          },
          {
            "text": "    Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 423
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 424
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 425
          },
          {
            "text": "fn run_builtin_core(",
            "lineNumber": 426
          },
          {
            "text": "    vfs: &amp;mut Vfs,",
            "lineNumber": 427
          },
          {
            "text": "    stdin: &amp;mut dyn Read,",
            "lineNumber": 428
          },
          {
            "text": "    stdout: &amp;mut dyn Write,",
            "lineNumber": 429
          },
          {
            "text": "    stderr: &amp;mut dyn Write,",
            "lineNumber": 430
          },
          {
            "text": "    argv: &amp;[String],",
            "lineNumber": 431
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; Result&lt;(), BuiltinError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 432
          },
          {
            "text": "    let name = argv.first().map_or(\"\", String::as_str);",
            "lineNumber": 433
          },
          {
            "text": "    match name {",
            "lineNumber": 434
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"pwd\" =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 435
          },
          {
            "text": "            writeln!(stdout, \"{}\", vfs.cwd()).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 436
          },
          {
            "text": "            Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 437
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 438
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"cd\" =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 439
          },
          {
            "text": "            let path = argv.get(1).map_or(\"/\", String::as_str);",
            "lineNumber": 440
          },
          {
            "text": "            vfs.set_cwd(path).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::CdFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 441
          },
          {
            "text": "            Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 442
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 443
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"ls\" =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 444
          },
          {
            "text": "            let path = argv.get(1).map_or(\".\", String::as_str);",
            "lineNumber": 445
          },
          {
            "text": "            let names = vfs.list_dir(path).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::LsFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 446
          },
          {
            "text": "            for n in names {",
            "lineNumber": 447
          },
          {
            "text": "                writeln!(stdout, \"{n}\").map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 448
          },
          {
            "text": "            }",
            "lineNumber": 449
          },
          {
            "text": "            Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 450
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 451
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"mkdir\" =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 452
          },
          {
            "text": "            let path = argv.get(1).ok_or(BuiltinError::MkdirFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 453
          },
          {
            "text": "            vfs.mkdir(path).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::MkdirFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 454
          },
          {
            "text": "            Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 455
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 456
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"cat\" =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 457
          },
          {
            "text": "            if argv.len() &lt;= 1 {",
            "lineNumber": 458
          },
          {
            "text": "                std::io::copy(stdin, stdout).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::CatFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 459
          },
          {
            "text": "            } else {",
            "lineNumber": 460
          },
          {
            "text": "                for path in argv.iter().skip(1) {",
            "lineNumber": 461
          },
          {
            "text": "                    let content = vfs.read_file(path).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::CatFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 462
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.4733012318611145
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/repl.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 99,
            "column": 1
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 148,
            "column": 20
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "fn save_on_exit&lt;W2: Write&gt;(vfs: &amp;Rc&lt;RefCell&lt;Vfs&gt;&gt;, bin_path: &amp;Path, stderr: &amp;mut W2) {",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "    if let Err(e) = serialization::save_to_file(&amp;vfs.borrow(), bin_path) {",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "        let _ = writeln!(stderr, \"save on exit failed: {e}\");",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "/// TTY branch: rustyline Editor with &#96;DevShellHelper&#96; (path completion via vfs).",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "fn run_tty&lt;R, W1, W2&gt;(",
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "text": "    vfs: &amp;Rc&lt;RefCell&lt;Vfs&gt;&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "    bin_path: &amp;Path,",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "text": "    stdin: &amp;mut R,",
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "    stdout: &amp;mut W1,",
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "text": "    stderr: &amp;mut W2,",
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; Result&lt;(), ()&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "text": "where",
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "text": "    R: BufRead + Read,",
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "text": "    W1: Write,",
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "    W2: Write,",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "text": "{",
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut editor = Editor::new().map_err(|_| ())?;",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "text": "    editor.set_helper(Some(DevShellHelper::new(vfs.clone())));",
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "text": "    loop {",
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "text": "        let prompt = format!(\"{} $ \", vfs.borrow().cwd());",
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "text": "        let line = match editor.readline(&amp;prompt) {",
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "text": "            Ok(line) =&gt; line,",
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "text": "            Err(rustyline::error::ReadlineError::Eof) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 128
          },
          {
            "text": "                save_on_exit(vfs, bin_path, stderr);",
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "text": "                return Ok(());",
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "text": "            }",
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "text": "            Err(rustyline::error::ReadlineError::Interrupted) =&gt; continue,",
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "text": "            Err(e) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 133
          },
          {
            "text": "                let _ = writeln!(stderr, \"readline error: {e}\");",
            "lineNumber": 134
          },
          {
            "text": "                continue;",
            "lineNumber": 135
          },
          {
            "text": "            }",
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "text": "        };",
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {
            "text": "        if process_line(vfs, &amp;line, stdin, stdout, stderr) == StepResult::Exit {",
            "lineNumber": 138
          },
          {
            "text": "            break;",
            "lineNumber": 139
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 141
          },
          {
            "text": "    save_on_exit(vfs, bin_path, stderr);",
            "lineNumber": 142
          },
          {
            "text": "    Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 143
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 144
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Non-TTY branch: &#96;read_line&#96; loop; &#96;borrow_mut&#96; once per iteration for prompt and &#96;ExecContext&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 146
          },
          {
            "text": "fn run_readline&lt;R, W1, W2&gt;(",
            "lineNumber": 147
          },
          {
            "text": "    vfs: &amp;Rc&lt;RefCell&lt;Vfs&gt;&gt;,",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 149

        ]

      "score": 0.4607304334640503
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/command.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 355,
            "column": 61
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 386,
            "column": 16
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "        \"search\" =&gt; run_todo_search(stdout, rest, &amp;list),",
            "lineNumber": 357
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"stats\" =&gt; run_todo_stats(stdout, &amp;list),",
            "lineNumber": 358
          },
          {
            "text": "        _ =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 359
          },
          {
            "text": "            writeln!(stderr, \"todo: unknown subcommand '{sub}' (list, add, show, update, complete, delete, search, stats)\")",
            "lineNumber": 360
          },
          {
            "text": "                .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 361
          },
          {
            "text": "            Err(BuiltinError::TodoArgError)",
            "lineNumber": 362
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 363
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 364
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 365
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 366
          },
          {
            "text": "fn run_builtin_help(stdout: &amp;mut dyn Write) -&gt; Result&lt;(), BuiltinError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 367
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(stdout, \"Supported commands:\").map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 368
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(stdout, \"  pwd              print current working directory\")",
            "lineNumber": 369
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 370
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(stdout, \"  cd &lt;path&gt;        change directory\")",
            "lineNumber": 371
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 372
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(stdout, \"  ls [path]        list directory contents\")",
            "lineNumber": 373
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 374
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(stdout, \"  mkdir &lt;path&gt;     create directory (and parents)\")",
            "lineNumber": 375
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 376
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(",
            "lineNumber": 377
          },
          {
            "text": "        stdout,",
            "lineNumber": 378
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"  cat [path...]    print file contents (or stdin if no path)\"",
            "lineNumber": 379
          },
          {
            "text": "    )",
            "lineNumber": 380
          },
          {
            "text": "    .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 381
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(stdout, \"  touch &lt;path&gt;     create empty file\")",
            "lineNumber": 382
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 383
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(stdout, \"  echo [args...]   print arguments\")",
            "lineNumber": 384
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 385
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(stdout, \"  save [path]      save virtual FS to .bin file\")",
            "lineNumber": 386
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_err",
            "lineNumber": 387
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.4525392949581146
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/mod.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 55,
            "column": 17
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "//! Devshell REPL and VFS: same logic as the &#96;cargo-devshell&#96; binary, exposed so tests can cover it.",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "pub mod command;",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "pub mod completion;",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "pub mod parser;",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "pub mod serialization;",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "pub mod todo_io;",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "pub mod vfs;",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "mod repl;",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::cell::RefCell;",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::io::{self, BufReader, IsTerminal};",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::path::Path;",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "use std::rc::Rc;",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "use vfs::Vfs;",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Error from &#96;run_with&#96; (usage or REPL failure).",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "#[derive(Debug)]",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "pub enum RunWithError {",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "    Usage,",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    ReplFailed,",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "impl std::fmt::Display for RunWithError {",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "    fn fmt(&amp;self, f: &amp;mut std::fmt::Formatter&lt;'_&gt;) -&gt; std::fmt::Result {",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "        match self {",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "            Self::Usage =&gt; f.write_str(\"usage error\"),",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "            Self::ReplFailed =&gt; f.write_str(\"repl failed\"),",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "impl std::error::Error for RunWithError {}",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Run the devshell using process args and standard I/O (for the binary).",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "///",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "/// # Errors",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Returns an error if usage is wrong or I/O fails critically.",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "pub fn run_main() -&gt; Result&lt;(), Box&lt;dyn std::error::Error&gt;&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    let args: Vec&lt;String&gt; = std::env::args().collect();",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "    let is_tty = io::stdin().is_terminal();",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut stdin = BufReader::new(io::stdin());",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut stdout = io::stdout();",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut stderr = io::stderr();",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "    run_main_from_args(&amp;args, is_tty, &amp;mut stdin, &amp;mut stdout, &amp;mut stderr)",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Same as &#96;run_main&#96; but takes args, &#96;is_tty&#96;, and streams (for tests and callers that supply I/O).",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "///",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "/// # Errors",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Returns an error if usage is wrong or I/O fails critically.",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "pub fn run_main_from_args&lt;R, W1, W2&gt;(",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "    args: &amp;[String],",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "    is_tty: bool,",
            "lineNumber": 56
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.4499549865722656
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/command.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 459,
            "column": 13
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 479,
            "column": 48
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": " else {",
            "lineNumber": 460
          },
          {
            "text": "                for path in argv.iter().skip(1) {",
            "lineNumber": 461
          },
          {
            "text": "                    let content = vfs.read_file(path).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::CatFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 462
          },
          {
            "text": "                    stdout",
            "lineNumber": 463
          },
          {
            "text": "                        .write_all(&amp;content)",
            "lineNumber": 464
          },
          {
            "text": "                        .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 465
          },
          {
            "text": "                }",
            "lineNumber": 466
          },
          {
            "text": "            }",
            "lineNumber": 467
          },
          {
            "text": "            Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 468
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 469
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"touch\" =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 470
          },
          {
            "text": "            let path = argv.get(1).ok_or(BuiltinError::TouchFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 471
          },
          {
            "text": "            vfs.touch(path).map_err(|_| BuiltinError::TouchFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 472
          },
          {
            "text": "            Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 473
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 474
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"echo\" =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 475
          },
          {
            "text": "            let line = argv[1..].join(\" \");",
            "lineNumber": 476
          },
          {
            "text": "            writeln!(stdout, \"{line}\").map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 477
          },
          {
            "text": "            Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 478
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 479
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"export-readonly\" | \"export_readonly\" =&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 480
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.4469110071659088
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/command.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 101,
            "column": 5
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 131,
            "column": 23
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "    if let Some(path) = stderr_redirect_path {",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "        if let Some(buf) = &amp;stderr_override {",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "            vfs.write_file(&amp;path, buf)",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "                .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "text": "    result",
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Apply redirects: build optional stdin (Cursor over file content), stdout/stderr buffers.",
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Then run the builtin with the effective streams; after that write stdout/stderr buffers to vfs if redirected.",
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "text": "///",
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "text": "/// # Errors",
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Returns &#96;BuiltinError&#96; on redirect or builtin execution failure.",
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "text": "pub fn run_builtin(ctx: &amp;mut ExecContext&lt;'_&gt;, cmd: &amp;SimpleCommand) -&gt; Result&lt;(), BuiltinError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "    run_builtin_with_streams(ctx.vfs, ctx.stdin, ctx.stdout, ctx.stderr, cmd)",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Execute a pipeline: run each command with stdin from previous stage (or &#96;ctx.stdin&#96; for first),",
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "/// stdout to a buffer; last command's stdout is written to &#96;ctx.stdout&#96;. Redirects override pipe.",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "text": "///",
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "text": "/// # Errors",
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Returns &#96;BuiltinError&#96; if any command or redirect fails.",
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "text": "///",
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "text": "/// # Panics",
            "lineNumber": 128
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Panics if the pipeline state is inconsistent (non-first stage without pipe input); this is a programming error.",
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "text": "pub fn execute_pipeline(",
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "text": "    ctx: &amp;mut ExecContext&lt;'_&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "text": "    pipeline: &amp;Pipeline",
            "lineNumber": 132
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.44051840901374817
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/command.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 48
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 103,
            "column": 43
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "fn run_builtin_with_streams(",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "    vfs: &amp;mut Vfs,",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "    default_stdin: &amp;mut dyn Read,",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "    default_stdout: &amp;mut dyn Write,",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "    default_stderr: &amp;mut dyn Write,",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "    cmd: &amp;SimpleCommand,",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; Result&lt;(), BuiltinError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "    let redirects = &amp;cmd.redirects;",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "    let argv = &amp;cmd.argv;",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut stdin_override: Option&lt;Cursor&lt;Vec&lt;u8&gt;&gt;&gt; = None;",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut stdout_override: Option&lt;Vec&lt;u8&gt;&gt; = None;",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut stderr_override: Option&lt;Vec&lt;u8&gt;&gt; = None;",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut stdout_redirect_path: Option&lt;String&gt; = None;",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut stderr_redirect_path: Option&lt;String&gt; = None;",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "    for r in redirects {",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "        match r.fd {",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "            0 =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "                let content = vfs",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "                    .read_file(&amp;r.path)",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "                    .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectRead)?;",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "                stdin_override = Some(Cursor::new(content));",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "            }",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "            1 =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "                stdout_override = Some(Vec::new());",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "                stdout_redirect_path = Some(r.path.clone());",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "            }",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "            2 =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "                stderr_override = Some(Vec::new());",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "                stderr_redirect_path = Some(r.path.clone());",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "            }",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "            _ =&gt; {}",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "    let stdin: &amp;mut dyn Read = stdin_override",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "text": "        .as_mut()",
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_or(default_stdin, |c| c as &amp;mut dyn Read);",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "    let stdout: &amp;mut dyn Write = stdout_override",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "        .as_mut()",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_or(default_stdout, |v| v as &amp;mut dyn Write);",
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "text": "    let stderr: &amp;mut dyn Write = stderr_override",
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "        .as_mut()",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "        .map_or(default_stderr, |v| v as &amp;mut dyn Write);",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "    let result = run_builtin_core(vfs, stdin, stdout, stderr, argv);",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "text": "    if let Some(path) = stdout_redirect_path {",
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "        if let Some(buf) = &amp;stdout_override {",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "            vfs.write_file(&amp;path, buf)",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "                .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "    if let Some(path) = stderr_redirect_path {",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "        if let Some(buf) = &amp;stderr_override",
            "lineNumber": 104
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.43344539403915405
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/command.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 327,
            "column": 34
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 357,
            "column": 49
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": ",",
            "lineNumber": 328
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; Result&lt;(), BuiltinError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 329
          },
          {
            "text": "    let (total, open, completed) = list.stats();",
            "lineNumber": 330
          },
          {
            "text": "    writeln!(",
            "lineNumber": 331
          },
          {
            "text": "        stdout,",
            "lineNumber": 332
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"open: {open}  completed: {completed}  total: {total}\"",
            "lineNumber": 333
          },
          {
            "text": "    )",
            "lineNumber": 334
          },
          {
            "text": "    .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;",
            "lineNumber": 335
          },
          {
            "text": "    Ok(())",
            "lineNumber": 336
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 337
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 338
          },
          {
            "text": "fn run_todo_cmd(",
            "lineNumber": 339
          },
          {
            "text": "    stdout: &amp;mut dyn Write,",
            "lineNumber": 340
          },
          {
            "text": "    stderr: &amp;mut dyn Write,",
            "lineNumber": 341
          },
          {
            "text": "    argv: &amp;[String],",
            "lineNumber": 342
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; Result&lt;(), BuiltinError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 343
          },
          {
            "text": "    let sub = argv.get(1).map_or(\"list\", String::as_str);",
            "lineNumber": 344
          },
          {
            "text": "    let rest = argv.get(2..).unwrap_or(&amp;[]);",
            "lineNumber": 345
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 346
          },
          {
            "text": "    let todos = load_todos().map_err(|_| BuiltinError::TodoLoadFailed)?;",
            "lineNumber": 347
          },
          {
            "text": "    let mut list = list_from_todos(todos);",
            "lineNumber": 348
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 349
          },
          {
            "text": "    match sub {",
            "lineNumber": 350
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"list\" =&gt; run_todo_list(stdout, &amp;list, rest),",
            "lineNumber": 351
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"add\" =&gt; run_todo_add(stdout, stderr, rest, &amp;mut list),",
            "lineNumber": 352
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"show\" =&gt; run_todo_show(stdout, rest, &amp;list),",
            "lineNumber": 353
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"update\" =&gt; run_todo_update(stderr, rest, &amp;mut list),",
            "lineNumber": 354
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"complete\" =&gt; run_todo_complete(stderr, rest, &amp;mut list),",
            "lineNumber": 355
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"delete\" =&gt; run_todo_delete(stderr, rest, &amp;mut list),",
            "lineNumber": 356
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"search\" =&gt; run_todo_search(stdout, rest, &amp;list),",
            "lineNumber": 357
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"stats\" =&gt; run_todo_stats(stdout, &amp;list),",
            "lineNumber": 358
          }
        ]
